1.description: Panel A depicts the log level of U.S. GDP from 1900 to 2023, while Panel B zooms in level from 1996 onwards. Shaded areas show recession periods.

2.description: Graphical illustration of the resilience measure. The horizontal dashed black line after the shock at  has zero resilience. If adaptation leads to the green process that bounces back, the resilience measure is positive as represented by the green shaded area. In contrast, if adaptation leads the process to further drift away after the shock, the resilience measure is negative as depicted by the red shaded area.

5.description: First-generation macrofinance (As in, for example, Kiyotaki and Moore (1997)).

6.description: Drift dynamics of stochastic process . The drift of the stochastic process  is log-linear only around the stochastic steady state of  with . When  drops below the tipping point, the drift becomes negative and  drifts further down toward the trap of an absorbing state at . The dotted  represents the  = 0-benchmark drift after a shock absent adjustment by the agents in the system. The top-right panel sketches the IRF after a shock to point , while the bottom-right panel depicts the IRF after the larger shock beyond the tipping point to .

7.description: Fan charts depicting the difference in evolution with and without a shock at .

8.description: Zero-cash-flow asset that agents A and B retrade serves as safe asset.
